UserProfile.title: User Profile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the application, defining their role and associations.

role.description: The user's role in the application.

adminId.description: If the user is a helper, this is the UID of their admin.

Nome.title: Nome
Nome.description: Representa um nome individual na lista de nomes do usuário.

id.description: Identificador único para a entidade Nome.

valor.description: O valor do nome adicionado à lista.

dataCriacao.description: Data e hora em que o nome foi adicionado pela primeira vez.

dataAtualizacao.description: Data e hora da última vez que o nome foi atualizado.

FieldGroup.title: Field Group
FieldGroup.description: Represents a group to categorize names.

createdAt.description: The date and time the group was created.

Invitation.title: Invitation
Invitation.description: Represents a pending invitation for a user to become a helper.

email.description: The email of the user being invited.

definition.description: Stores the profile information for each user, including their role and admin association if they are a helper.
Each document represents an individual name in a user's list. The `userId` is the owner of the data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this list of names.

params.name: nomeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific name within the user's list.

definition.description: Represents a group created by a user to categorize their names. The `userId` is the owner of the group.
Stores pending invitations sent by an admin to potential helpers.

firestore.reasoning: The data structure now includes a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection to store user profiles with role-based access control information (`role`, `adminId`). Data collections like `nomes` and `fieldGroups` remain nested under `/users/{ownerId}` to maintain clear data ownership. A new `invitations` sub-collection is added to manage the helper invitation workflow. This structure allows for secure, delegated access using Firestore's path-based security and `get()` calls in rules to verify helper status.
